feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: U.S. and world leaders pay tribute to Lindsey Graham following sudden death
entry.content: The senior U.S. senator for South Carolina died suddenly at 71 on Saturday.

entry.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 12)
entry.content: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

entry.title: Almanac: July 12
entry.content: "Sunday Morning" looks back at historical events on this date.

entry.title: How white supremacists staged the only successful coup in U.S. history
entry.content: In 1898, Wilmington, N.C., was prosperous and integrated. But white supremacists took back control of the city's multi-racial government at gunpoint, and killed scores of Black residents - a little-known story retold in Lauren Collins' "They Stole a City."

entry.title: How white supremacists staged the only successful coup in U.S. history
entry.content: In 1898, Wilmington, N.C., was a prosperous integrated city, where Black and white North Carolinians shared political power and leadership positions. But white supremacists took back control of the city's multi-racial government at gunpoint, and launched a wave of violence that killed scores of Black residents. That little-known history is the subject of The New Yorker journalist Lauren Collins' new book, "They Stole a City." She talks with Lee Cowan about her hometown's darkest chapter – the only successful coup in our nation's history.

entry.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies after "brief and sudden illness"
entry.content: President Trump paid tribute to the late senator, who was reportedly scheduled to do an interview on Sunday.

entry.title: Goldman Sachs picks its favorite Chinese AI models
entry.content: The investment bank released its Chinese AI model competitive positioning framework on Friday.

entry.title: ‘I felt dizzy’: bodybuilder recalls how drug abuse caught up with him
entry.content: Jamie Mantzouridis used steroids, growth hormones and insulin until the side effects became impossible to ignoreFitness influencers linked to wellness brand helping run illegal steroid market on TelegramJamie Mantzouridis vividly remembers the first time he took steroids. He got someone else to inject him and his mind was racing. “What if he hit an artery? What if there was an air bubble?” Mantzouridis, however, says he was young and fearless.He was small growing up, skinny and self-conscious. By the time he started training seriously in his early 20s, he noticed other guys at the gym who were much larger, and they explained they were taking steroids. “They looked really good,” he says, “and they said that was what they were doing.” Continue reading...

entry.title: UK becoming ‘wild west’ for experimental peptides, expert warns
entry.content: Prof Channa Jayasena says growing online sales of unregulated drugs risk fatalities as responsibility falls between regulatorsFitness influencers linked to wellness brand helping run illegal steroid market on TelegramThe UK has become a “wild west” for people peddling experimental peptides, steroids and other substances, a leading expert has said, warning action must be taken to avoid fatalities.Prof Channa Jayasena of Imperial College London, a consultant in reproductive endocrinology and andrology at Hammersmith and St Mary’s hospitals, said he is now encountering patients “day in, day out” who are taking experimental peptides. Continue reading...

entry.title: Fitness influencers linked to wellness brand helping run illegal steroid market on Telegram
entry.content: Ambassadors for Gencore Global directed followers to Telegram channels promoting steroids, prescription medicines and experimental peptides‘I felt dizzy’: body builder recalls how drug abuse caught up with himUK becoming ‘wild west’ for experimental peptides, expert warnsFitness influencers who publicly represent a global wellness brand are involved in running an illegal steroid market on social media, the Guardian can reveal.Gencore Global presents itself as a UK-based health and wellness company and has recently appeared at FitXpo North West, a fitness event in Greater Manchester. It has also sponsored a racehorse, launched a UK combat sports and influencer boxing promotion, and is set to attend the National Running Show in Birmingham next year. Continue reading...

entry.title: MLB Futures Game rosters 2026: Full list of prospects playing includes Leo De Vries, Eli Willits
entry.content: MLB's next wave of talent will take the field in Philadelphia for the Futures Game. Here are the full rosters.

entry.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71 after ‘brief and sudden illness’
entry.content: Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., who was elected to the Senate in 2003 and was a close political ally of President Donald Trump, has died, his office confirmed early Sunday. He was 71.

entry.title: Switzerland manager criticises controversial new ‘mistaken identity’ rule after World Cup exit
entry.content: Switzerland’s first quarter-final in 72 years ended with Argentina instead booking a date with England in the last four

entry.title: Barcelona’s latest signing won’t impact pursuit of La Liga sensation – Romano
entry.content: Barcelona have moved quickly to secure a move for talented German attacker Karim Adeyemi from Borussia Dortmund.Much like the Anthony Gordon deal that they pulled off earlier this summer, there was li...

entry.title: Norway talisman heaps praise on Jude Bellingham after World Cup quarterfinal clash: ‘Real Madrid and England are lucky’
entry.content: Jude Bellingham is rewriting the history books with his FIFA World Cup campaign, and he is even a strong contender for the Golden Ball at this point in time.Taking to the field against Norway in Engla...

entry.title: This is ‘hell’ – Conor McGregor breaks silence on UFC 329 loss, claims no pre-fight injury
entry.content: Conor McGregor has issued his first statement since losing at UFC 329.

entry.title: World Cup QF Drama: Argentina Beats Swiss, England Overcomes Norway Cable Controversy & Semis Preview
entry.content: Christian Polanco and Alexis Guerreros go late-night on the ground in Miami to deliver a comprehensive live reaction to a wild day of World Cup quarterfinal action. The guys break down the regulatory madness of Breel Embolo's simulation red card in Argentina's tight 3-1 win over Switzerland, alongside the twin controversies of overturned goals and stadium skycam cable strikes during England's grueling 2-1 extra-time win over Norway. Evaluating standout masterclasses from Julián Álvarez and Jude Bellingham against an isolated, hobbling Erling Haaland, the hosts hand out final tournament grades to the exiting underdogs before dropping official score predictions for the locked-in Spain vs. France and England vs. Argentina semifinal blockbusters.

entry.title: U.S. conducts another round of retaliatory strikes on Iran, Pentagon says
entry.content: U.S. forces conducted their third round of strikes on Iran this week, this time in retaliation for an attack on a commercial ship in the Strait of Hormuz, the Pentagon said.

entry.title: Record breaker crosses Iceland in 'best-ever time'
entry.content: Jack Pitcher smashed the British record for travelling across Australia by foot in 2024.

entry.title: Whipsnakes bounce back with dominant offensive showing in win over Chaos
entry.content: The Whipsnakes had some soul-searching to do following their loss to the Denver Outlaws two weeks ago. Maryland’s offense was subpar, and its second-half execution meant it was handed a...

entry.title: Kansas woman convicted of double murder after three trials
entry.content: Mike Sisco and his girlfriend Karen Harkness were gunned down in her Topeka, Kansas, home in 2002. Authorities believed it was a crime of passion. Sisco's daughter set out to help prove it was her mother, Dana Chandler, who was responsible.

entry.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
entry.content: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

entry.title: Who is still in the World Cup? Updated list of teams left in 2026 FIFA semifinal bracket
entry.content: Here's a look at the four sides left in this year's competiton.

entry.title: UFC 329: Paddy Pimblett makes statement with quick submission victory
entry.content: While Conor McGregor took the headlines, Paddy Pimblett actually earned them at UFC 329.

entry.title: Where to watch MLB All-Star Futures Game: TV channel, live stream, time for baseball prospects showcase
entry.content: The Sporting News has all the details on how to watch the 2026 MLB All-Star Futures Game.

entry.title: My Mother's Murder Trials - Part 2
entry.content: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports for "48 Hours."

entry.title: Switzerland's well-earned reward for acting is ejection from game and elimination from World Cup
entry.content: Switzerland striker Breel Embolo delivered some convincing acting, but this wasn't a Broadway play. This was the World Cup. And with video review now so prominent in the sport, that gets you punished, not rewarded.

entry.title: Soccer world reacts to controversial red card that helped Argentina beat Switzerland, advance to World Cup semis
entry.content: Referee Joao Pinheiro's decision to send Breel Embolo off for "mistaken identity" left many onlookers feeling confused.

entry.title: Will Conor McGregor retire? Knee injury suffered vs, Max Holloway makes legend's future appear uncertain
entry.content: Here's the latest on whether or not McGregor will definitively close out his career with his latest injury.

entry.title: Social media reacts to Conor McGregor injuring knee in opening moments of return fight vs. Max Holloway
entry.content: Conor McGregor's attempt at a return ended as soon as the fight started. The Sporting News compiled reactions to the fight at UFC 329, highlighting discussions about McGregor's injury.

entry.title: A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit Venezuela
entry.content: Discontent with Trump-backed government mounts as Chávez heirs struggle to respond to disaster for which they seem ill-preparedEven before two powerful earthquakes reduced the OPPE 25 government housing project to an anarchy of shattered concrete and broken lives, the foundations of Hugo Chávez’s populist “Bolivarian” revolution were shaking in what was once a hotbed of support.Gabriel González remembers his elation when, in 2013, he received the keys to his freshly completed apartment in one of the 12-floor tower blocks El Comandante had ordered to be built in an affluent corner of the resort town of Caraballeda. Continue reading...

entry.title: Toronto shooting: two dead and four injured at Salsa on St Clair street festival
entry.content: Police say two people exchanged gunfire in shooting that mayor called an ‘irresponsible act of violence’ in festival attended by familiesA shooting near a Toronto street festival killed two men and wounded four other people on Saturday evening, police said, adding that what initially prompted an active-shooter warning was an exchange of gunfire between two people targeting each other.Toronto police deputy chief Frank Barredo said investigators recovered two firearms after the shooting, which was reported at 8.12pm near St. Clair Avenue West and Arlington Avenue, where the Salsa on St Clair festival was underway. Continue reading...

entry.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entry.content: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entry.title: At least 2 killed in shooting at Toronto street festival, police say
entry.content: At least two people were killed and several more wounded in a shooting Saturday evening at a street festival in Toronto, Canada, authorities said.

entry.title: A look at the Missouri flooding that left over 200 campers stranded
entry.content: Six to 12 inches of rain caused historic flooding in Missouri this week. Over 200 people were stranded at a children's camp where all roads were cut off by floodwaters. Rescue crews airlifted them to safety with eight Black Hawk helicopters.

entry.title: Wagner’s Remnants Are Running an Opioid Empire in the Center of Africa
entry.content: Russia absorbed much of the mercenary group, but several hundred have used the painkiller tramadol to build a new haven in the Central African Republic.

entry.title: U.S. Launches New Strikes on Iran in Renewed Standoff Over Hormuz
entry.content: The fresh strikes were a response to a Revolutionary Guard attack on a containership in the strait, officials say.

entry.title: Aspiring to Regional Domination, Iran Is Ready to Escalate Over Hormuz
entry.content: A fresh round of fighting over the key waterway highlights that for Tehran, keeping a chokehold on the strait cements its new power in the region.

entry.title: England defeats Norway for World Cup semifinals spot
entry.content: In extra time, England defeated Norway to clinch a spot in the World Cup semifinals. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch reports.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The older you get, the better you were.
Lucky Numbers: 4, 63, 60, 48, 55, 9

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Do something unusual when locking the door before a long trip to remember that you've definitely done it.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Americans on the average eat 18 acres of pizza every day.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Gustave Flaubert
entry.content: “Success is a consequence and must not be a goal.” — Gustave Flaubert

entry.title: Guggenheim museum in New York City tests positive for legionnaires’ disease
entry.content: Famed art museum, one of 31 buildings to test positive, has already completed remediation, health department saysNew York City’s famed Solomon R Guggenheim Museum was among a number of Manhattan buildings that recently tested positive for the bacteria that causes legionnaires’ disease.The city health department on Friday released a list of 31 buildings on the Upper East Side that have been ordered to clean and disinfect their cooling towers as the city deals with the latest outbreak of the disease, which is a serious form of pneumonia. Continue reading...

entry.title: ‘All smiles.’ Mariners select college slugger Ace Reese in first round of MLB Draft
entry.content: “I definitely had (the Mariners) on my list of teams I wanted to go to, so I was pumped when I got that opportunity,” Reese said.

entry.title: Jake Schaffner drafted by Boston Red Sox in 1st round of MLB Draft
entry.content: Former North Carolina shortstop Jake Schaffner became the 26th Tar Heel taken in the first round when the Boston Red Sox selected him No. 20 overall.

entry.title: Collegiate baseball: Rapids generate big rally against Genesee to win season home finale
entry.content: WATERTOWN — Even when they’re down, the Watertown Rapids have rarely considered themselves out for most of their challenging season. Despite blowing an early lead, the Rapids responded by finding a way to finally break through for a victory, winning in their season home finale Friday night. After the Genesee Rapids won the first game of the day, 9-3, Watertown bounced back to prevail, 10-8, in ...

entry.title: Dodgers draft Bo Lowrance with 1st pick, 40th overall
entry.content: The Dodgers began their 2026 MLB Draft on Saturday by selecting Bo Lowrance, a high school third baseman from South Carolina with their first-round pick, and 40th overall in the draft. By virtue of winning the World Series last year, the Dodgers owned the 30th pick in the first round this year. But by exceeding […]

entry.title: Ole Miss signee Cole Prosek picked by Chicago White Sox in 2026 MLB Draft

entry.title: Poll: Americans split on capitalism, skeptical of democracy
entry.content: Americans' views on capitalism are split but lean negative, according to a new survey. Just over half of Americans, 51%, believe capitalism is functioning "not too well" or "not at all well," while 48% say it is functioning "very well" or "somewhat well."

entry.content: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration has approved plans to relocate 28 beluga whales from a closed Canadian theme park.

entry.title: When is Wimbledon men’s final? Jannik Sinner v Alexander Zverev start time and how to watch
entry.content: Sinner will attempt to win consecutive Wimbledon titles while Zverev is out to win a second grand slam title in a row

entry.title: McGlynn flattered by Scotland links but focused on Falkirk
entry.content: Falkirk boss John McGlynn is flattered to be touted for the Scotland job but insists he is fully focused on the Bairns. After back-to-back promotions that saw Falkirk rise from League One to the Scottish Premiership in two years, the 64-year-old led the side to a top-six finish in their first season back in the top flight. Given the impressive job McGlynn has done, former Scotland boss Craig Levein suggested McGlynn would be an excellent pick to succeed Steve Clarke as the national team manager.

entry.title: Why a two-week transformation now poses Wimbledon final’s biggest question
entry.content: Jannik Sinner, the world No 1 and reigning Wimbledon champion, has a dominant record against Alexander Zverev, but has becoming a grand slam winner changed everything for the German?

entry.content: Kevin De Bruyne has reassured Napoli supporters that he will come back stronger next season, hours after Belgium’s World Cup ended in quarter-final heartbreak.The Red Devils bowed out following a ti...

entry.title: Meta removes AI feature that drew from users' public Instagram content
entry.content: Meta has axed a feature in its new AI image generation model that allowed users on Instagram to create images drawing on the publicly available media of other users.

entry.title: Dating and immigration: Apps don't ask for a green card, and some men are banking on that
entry.content: Dating is hard enough without throwing illegal immigration into the equation.

entry.title: Could birthright citizenship be the next Roe v. Wade?
entry.content: It took nearly 50 years for conservatives to see Roe v. Wade overturned. They're hoping it won't take that long to nix Trump v. Barbara, last month's decision on birthright citizenship.

entry.title: Unsealed letter reveals Scalia's fierce defense of landmark religious liberty decision
entry.content: Justice Antonin Scalia usually let his opinions speak for themselves, but he was so irked by criticism of one of his religious liberty rulings that he wrote a private defense of his reasoning, according to an unsealed court document obtained by "Seen, Heard & Whispered."

entry.title: Apple files lawsuit accusing ChatGPT maker OpenAI of stealing trade secrets
entry.content: Apple on Friday accused OpenAI of stealing trade secrets as it seeks to build its own hardware for ChatGPT, a major rupture in a partnership between the iPhone maker and the artificial intelligence company.

entry.title: Japan's space agency conducts first test flight for experimental reusable rocket
entry.content: Japan's experimental reusable rocket took off and safely landed in a first test flight Saturday as the country seeks to achieve the technology key to cut launch costs and compete in the global space market dominated by SpaceX.
